Just now

--

If someone mentions industrial innovation, people imagine robots, artificial intelligence, and production line automation replacing people’s work. Though these innovations dramatically transform the process, they do not remove the necessity of people — they just transform their work.
The most effective digital transformation programs are based not on replacement but on empowering workers.
This idea lies behind the emergence of the connected workforce concept.
What Is the Connected Workforce?
Connected workforce is the concept implying that employees receive access to real-time information, digital solutions and connected systems helping them work more effectively and safely.
Instead of using traditional paper documents, manual reports or any other means of outdated communication, employees can obtain operational information in real time.
It leads to much quicker interaction among production teams, maintenance staff, warehouse workers and managers.
Why Information is Important on the Factory Floor
Industrial processes are fast-paced.
Late equipment alerts for maintenance could mean a problem could turn into a big breakdown. Late information regarding inventory levels could affect production planning. Late visibility on the part of employees regarding changing circumstances could pose safety hazards.
Having workers get timely information will help them make decisions without having to wait for manual reporting and multiple approvals.
Technology is used not as a barrier but as an enabler.
Technologies Used to Help Create a Connected Workforce
Organizations are increasingly adopting technologies that promote effective communication and increased visibility within the industrial environment.
Some examples include:

• Mobile workforce software

• Digital work instructions

• Intelligent wearables

• Maintenance platforms

• Operational dashboards powered by AI

• Real-time asset management software
These allow workers to spend less time trying to gather information and more time fixing problems.
Collaboration Is Turning into a Competitive Edge
Industrial companies usually consist of a number of different departments that have different functions and goals.
Production crews concentrate on production.
Maintenance crews concentrate on reliability.
Warehouse crews concentrate on inventory management.
Managers concentrate on performance.
If all data is kept within departmental boundaries, effective collaboration becomes impossible.
Technologies for the connected workforce make it possible to share a single view of the processes, which makes collaboration easier and more efficient.
Improvement of Safety through Connected Technologies
The issue of safety is probably one of the topmost important issues of any industrial environment.
With connected technologies, it is possible to provide:

• Immediate machine alerts

• Electronic safety checklists

• Environmental monitoring in real time

• Rapid incident notification

• Visibility of dangerous situations
This doesn’t mean that these technologies substitute traditional safety measures, but only improve them.
Preparing Employees for Digital Transformation
Technology on its own does not ensure success.
Organizations have to spend resources on training their employees and continuous education.
The most successful digital transformation initiatives usually include encouraging employees to:

• Familiarize themselves with the new technologies

• Work in cross-departmental teams

• Share knowledge and practices

• Stay flexible when it comes to changing technologies
Employees’ support and encouragement play a crucial role in the successful implementation of changes.
The Future
With constant development of AI, IoT, and automation technologies, people’s role in industrial organizations will evolve as well.
Some routine jobs might be automated with time; however, critical thinking, problem solving, collaboration and decision making skills will stay relevant for years to come.
Industrial innovation of the future is all about cooperation of people and technology. The question here is not about choosing sides.
Readers interested in the way connected technologies, AI and IoT help to foster innovations in industrial businesses can find more information at Aperture Venture Studio website.
Closing Thoughts
Technological evolution can be gauged in terms of innovation, but the sustainable development of such transformations relies upon the individuals using them.
Companies that harness the power of interconnected solutions, timely information, and useful technological resources will stand a greater chance of boosting their productivity, safety, and future adaptability.
Ultimately, technology creates chances, but people make things happen.
